essential_oils_reference.name: Amyris
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Soft, resinous aroma similar to Benzoin
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Used as alternative to Sandalwood, helps 'fix' soap scents

essential_oils_reference.name: Bergamot
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Clean and refreshing citrus, used in Earl Grey Tea
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: One of the few top-note EOs that can be used alone in soap

essential_oils_reference.name: Black Pepper
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Use sparingly - start with few drops, blend with other oils

essential_oils_reference.name: Cardamom
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Works best in blends, especially with citrus, spice, and woodsy scents

essential_oils_reference.name: Cedarwood
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Blends well with floral, spice, and wood oils

essential_oils_reference.name: Chamomile (German)
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Strong, floral-herbal, less apple-scented than Roman
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Blue in color (blue chamomile), expensive - mainly for leave-on products

essential_oils_reference.name: Chamomile (Roman)
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Sweet, fruity, and floral - reminiscent of apples
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Blends especially well with floral and citrus oils

essential_oils_reference.name: Citronella
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Fresh and citrusy, similar to but more powerful than lemongrass
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Holds fragrance well in soap, often used in bug-repellent formulations

essential_oils_reference.name: Clary Sage
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Soft, herbaceous, earthy with slight sweetness - earthier lavender
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Does better in blends, especially with floral, woodsy, and citrus

essential_oils_reference.name: Eucalyptus
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Associated with medicinal products, does well in blends with citrus

essential_oils_reference.name: Fennel (Sweet)
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Best used in blends due to low usage rate

essential_oils_reference.name: Geranium
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Floral, earthy, and deep - rose geranium
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Often used instead of rose EO (less expensive), use alone or in blends

essential_oils_reference.name: Ginger
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: May smell different from fresh ginger, use in blends with deep scents

essential_oils_reference.name: Grapefruit
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Use folded (5x) for stronger scent that lasts

essential_oils_reference.name: Jasmine
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Best used in blends due to low usage rate

essential_oils_reference.name: Juniper Berry
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Low usage rate, tendency to fade - not very popular

essential_oils_reference.name: Lavender (English)
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Long used in perfume industry, blends well with many other EOs

essential_oils_reference.name: Lemongrass
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Does well alone or blended
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Can cause soap to trace quickly

essential_oils_reference.name: Lemon
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Fresh, strong, sharp lemon like zested peel
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Use folded (5x or 10x) for long-lasting scent
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Ordinary lemon EO does not last well in soap

essential_oils_reference.name: Lime
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Only use distilled lime EO (not cold-pressed) - can be irritating
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Scent may fade relatively quickly, use only distilled

essential_oils_reference.name: Marjoram (Sweet)
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Warm and peppery, similar to oregano
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Not common in soapmaking but blends with herbal, citrus, floral

essential_oils_reference.name: May Chang
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: One of the BEST citrus EOs for soapmaking - long-lasting, wonderful alone or in blends
essential_oils_reference.color_effect: Slightly yellow - can tint or warm soap colors

essential_oils_reference.name: Melissa
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Sweet, herbal, fresh, lemony, and light
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Expensive - affordable options often diluted

essential_oils_reference.name: Neroli
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Floral honey scent from bitter orange flowers
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Light and beautiful fragrance
essential_oils_reference.note: Top, Middle, or Base (depends on blend)

essential_oils_reference.name: Orange
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Use folded (5x or 10x) for lasting fragrance
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Ordinary orange EO does not last well in soap

essential_oils_reference.name: Palmarosa
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Lightly floral, rose-like with citrusy and grassy undertones
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Also called gingergrass

essential_oils_reference.name: Patchouli
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Can be used alone but broader appeal when blended

essential_oils_reference.name: Peppermint
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Sharp and filled with herbal menthol
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Can be used alone or in blends with herbal or citrus oils

essential_oils_reference.name: Petitgrain
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: From bitter orange tree bark, best in blends

essential_oils_reference.name: Rose Absolute
essential_oils_reference.botanical_name: Rosa damascena or Rosa x Centifolia
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Very expensive, sold pure or 5% diluted, restricted due to methyl eugenol allergen content
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Very low usage rate due to allergen content

essential_oils_reference.name: Rose Otto
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Attar of Roses, steam distilled - NOT worth using in soap due to extremely low rate
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Usage rate so low it's not practical for soap

essential_oils_reference.name: Rosemary
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Blends well with floral, herbal, and citrus

essential_oils_reference.name: Rosewood
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Also called Bois de Rose, use in blends with wood and floral scents

essential_oils_reference.name: Sandalwood
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Expensive with sustainability issues - know your source
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Beware of synthetic fragrance oils sold as sandalwood EO

essential_oils_reference.name: Scots Pine
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Blends with herbal, woodsy, and citrus
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Scent can be short-lasting in soap

essential_oils_reference.name: Spearmint
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Gentler than peppermint but lower usage rate due to carvone content
essential_oils_reference.warnings: Very low usage rate due to natural carvone content

essential_oils_reference.name: Tea Tree
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Ranges from sweet to sharp, camphorous to citrusy
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Clean medicinal scent, often used for oily skin/acne - a little goes a long way

essential_oils_reference.name: Vetiver
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Related to lemongrass, blend with floral oils and deep scents

essential_oils_reference.name: Ylang Ylang II or III
essential_oils_reference.scent_profile: Sweet, delicate, deep, tropically floral - Flower of Flowers
essential_oils_reference.usage_notes: Grades: Super Extra/Extra/I (perfume) vs II/III (soapmaking). Use in blends.

usage_notes.ppo_definition: PPO means Per Pound of Oils (454g) in your main soap recipe
usage_notes.safety_source: Conservative maximum rates from comprehensive CPSR assessment
usage_notes.folded_oils: Citrus oils available in 5x or 10x concentrations for longer-lasting scent

scent_notes.top: Evaporates quickly, first scent noticed (15-30 min)
